judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong margins and excellent cash flow, trading at a reasonable forward multiple despite a high trailing P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 1.3%; next earnings date is 2026-06-03.
positives: Revenue growth 29.5% YoY and strong analyst estimates for current Q (51.3%) and FY (66.6%).
Excellent margins with Gross margin at 76.7%, Operating margin at 44.9%, and Profit margin at 36.6%.
Strong free cash flow of $25.50B TTM supports operations and dividends.
Forward P/E of 22.88 is attractive given the growth profile and PEG ratio of 0.91.
Consensus is strong buy with a mean analyst target of $480.49.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 81.28 and Price/Book of 24.77 indicate a high valuation on historical metrics.
Total debt of $66.06B is significant, though offset by substantial cash and free cash flow.
Beta of 1.44 suggests higher volatility relative to the broader market.
Challenging EU regulators could introduce regulatory risk.

news.paragraph: Broadcom is reportedly challenging EU regulators while Barclays sees AI upside in the stock, as the company is also being considered a top long-term investment. This comes amidst broader market movements with Dow Jones futures falling and Nvidia showing some wavering. The next earnings date is set for June 3rd, which could be a catalyst for price action.

headlines.title: Dow Jones Futures Fall As Oil Prices Top $100; Nvidia Wavers, Walmart Falls, SpaceX Files For Huge IPO
Nvidia Tells Skeptical Investors AI Is Ready to Go Mainstream
Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Challenges EU Regulators While Barclays Sees AI Upside
Is Broadcom (AVGO) One of the Best Long Term US Stocks to Buy Right Now?
